matches.rationale: Same Bash action and pre-PR library context; the extracted rule explicitly requires `make format` in the modified library's directory. It also bundles lint and test, but under the 1:1 rule it is assigned to this single closest constituent gold rule.
Same Edit target: the pattern detects Sphinx-style double-backtick inline code in Python edits, and the evidence expressly prohibits it in docstrings and comments.

misses.note: `make lint` is present only inside the bundled `pre-pr-lib-checks` extraction, which is already consumed by g1; strict 1:1 matching leaves g2 unmatched.
`make test` is present only inside the bundled `pre-pr-lib-checks` extraction, which is already consumed by g1; strict 1:1 matching leaves g3 unmatched.
Compiler self-reported this skip as uncompilable: "TEST=path/to/test.py make test 로 특정 테스트 파일·pytest 옵션을 지정하는 방법 안내 — 사용법 문서일 뿐 의무가 부여되지 않음".

The single-backtick requirement appears only in the evidence of `no-sphinx-double-backtick`; that extracted rule's trigger pattern targets double backticks and is already consumed by the closer prohibition rule g5.
